name: mallm
documentation:
MALLM

Multi-Agent LLMs For Conversational Task-Solving: Framework

Report Bug · Request Feature

## Install Create an environment with: `conda create --name mallm python=3.12` ### Package Install as a package: `pip install -e .` ### Create Data Download and create the test data: `python data/data_downloader.py --datasets=[SQuAD2,ETPC]` You can use any dataset for this project as long as it follows [this basic format](https://github.com/Multi-Agent-LLMs/mallm/blob/main/data/datasets/etpc_debugging.json). These datasets are supported by our automated formatting pipeline: `BBQGenderIdentity`, `BTVote`, `ETHICS`, `ETPC`, `Europarl`, `GPQA`, `GSM8K`, `IFEval`, `MMLU`, `MMLUPro`, `MUSR`, `MathLvl5`, `MoCaMoral`, `MoralExceptQA`, `MultiNews`, `SQuAD2`, `SimpleEthicalQuestions`, `StrategyQA`, `WMT19DeEn`, `WinoGrande`, `XSum` ### Run from Terminal MALLM relies on an external API like OpenAI or Text Generation Inference by Huggingface. Once the endpoint is available, you can initiate all discussions with a single script. Example with TGI: `python mallm/scheduler.py --input_json_file_path=data/datasets/etpc_debugging.json --output_json_file_path=test_out.json --task_instruction_prompt="Paraphrase the input text." --endpoint_url="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1"` Or with OpenAI: `python mallm/scheduler.py --input_json_file_path=data/datasets/etpc_debugging.json --output_json_file_path=test_out.json --task_instruction_prompt="Paraphrase the input text." --endpoint_url="https://api.openai.com/v1" --api_key=""` ## Run command line scripts You can run the command line scripts from the terminal. The following command will run the scheduler with the given parameters: `mallm-run --input_json_file_path=data/datasets/etpc_debugging.json --output_json_file_path=test_out.json --task_instruction_prompt="Paraphrase the input text." --endpoint_url="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1" --model_name="tgi"` or use the evaluation script: `mallm-evaluate --input_json_file_path=test_out.json --output_json_file_path=test_out_evaluated.json --metrics=[bleu,rouge]` ## Run as Module If installed, you can use MALLM in code: ```py from mallm import scheduler from mallm.utils.config import Config mallm_scheduler = scheduler.Scheduler( Config( input_json_file_path="data/datasets/etpc_debugging.json", output_json_file_path="test_out.json", task_instruction_prompt="Paraphrase the input text.", endpoint_url="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1" ) ) mallm_scheduler.run() ``` ## Code Structure MALLM is composed of three parts. The framework follows this structure and can be found in the `mallm` directory. 1) Agents (subdirectory: `mallm/agents/`) 2) Discourse Policy (subdirectory: `mallm/discourse_policy/`) 3) Decision Protocol (subdirectory: `mallm/decision_protocol/`) Experiments can be implemented as a separate repository, loading MALLM as a package. ## Arguments ### Config Arguments: ```py input_json_file_path: str = None output_json_file_path: str = None task_instruction_prompt: str = None task_instruction_prompt_template: Optional[str] = None endpoint_url: str = "https://api.openai.com/v1" model_name: str = "gpt-3.5-turbo" api_key: str = "-" max_turns: int = 10 skip_decision_making: bool = False discussion_paradigm: str = "memory" response_generator: str = "simple" decision_protocol: str = "hybrid_consensus" visible_turns_in_memory: int = 2 debate_rounds: int = 2 concurrent_api_requests: int = 100 use_baseline: bool = False use_chain_of_thought: bool = True num_agents: int = 3 num_neutral_agents: int = 0 agent_generator: str = "expert" agent_generators_list: list = [] trust_remote_code: bool = False num_samples: Optional[int] = None hf_dataset_split: Optional[str] = "test" hf_token: Optional[str] = None hf_dataset_version: Optional[str] = None hf_dataset_input_column: Optional[str] = None hf_dataset_reference_column: Optional[str] = None hf_dataset_context_column: Optional[str] = None use_ablation: bool = False shuffle_input_samples: bool = False all_agents_generate_first_draft: bool = False all_agents_generate_draft: bool = False policy: Optional[str] = None voting_protocols_with_alterations: bool = False calculate_persona_diversity: bool = False challenge_final_results: bool = False ``` ### Discussion Parameters: Response Generators: `freetext`, `json`, `simple`, `splitfreetext` Decision Protocols: `approval_voting`, `consensus_voting`, `cumulative_voting`, `hybrid_consensus`, `majority_consensus`, `ranked_voting`, `simple_voting`, `summary`, `supermajority_consensus`, `unanimity_consensus` Persona Generators: `expert`, `ipip`, `mock`, `nopersona` Discussion Paradigms: `collective_refinement`, `debate`, `memory`, `relay`, `report` ## Evaluation We provide some basic evaluation metrics that can be directly applied to the output json of mallm. Supported metrics: `answerability`, `bertscore`, `bleu`, `ifeval`, `includes_answer`, `meteor`, `multichoice`, `rouge`, `squad` From terminal: `mallm-evaluate --input_json_file_path=test_out.json --output_json_file_path=test_out_evaluated.json --metrics=[bleu,rouge]` From script: ```py from mallm.evaluation.evaluator import Evaluator evaluator = Evaluator(input_file_path="test_out.json", metrics=["bleu", "rouge"], extensive=False) evaluator.process() ``` ## Logging To enable logging you can add a handler to the library logger. This can be done with the following code ```py import logging # Configure logging for the library library_logger = logging.getLogger("mallm") library_logger.setLevel(logging.INFO) # Add handlers to the logger stream_handler = logging.StreamHandler() # Optionally set a formatter formatter = logging.Formatter('%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s') stream_handler.setFormatter(formatter) # Attach the handler to the logger library_logger.addHandler(stream_handler) ``` ## Using the Batch Executor The batch executor allows you to run multiple configurations of the MALLM (Multi-Agent Language Model) scheduler in sequence. This is useful for running experiments with different parameters or processing multiple datasets. ### Location - The batch executor [script](https://github.com/Multi-Agent-LLMs/mallm/blob/main/mallm/scripts/batch_mallm.py) is located in the `mallm/scripts` folder and is named `batch_mallm.py`. - A [template](https://github.com/Multi-Agent-LLMs/mallm/tree/main/mallm/scripts/batch.json.template) for the batch configuration file is provided as `batch.json.template` in the same folder. ### Setup 1. **Prepare your configuration file:** - Copy the `batch.json.template` file and rename it (e.g., `my_batch_config.json`). - Edit the JSON file to define your configurations. The file has four main sections: - `name`: A descriptive name for the batch of runs. This is optional but will be added to the output filename and can help identify the purpose of the batch. - `repeats`: The number of times to repeat each run. This is useful for running multiple trials with the same configuration. - `common`: Contains settings that apply to all runs unless overridden. - `runs`: An array of run-specific configurations. Example: ```json { "name": "test", "repeats": 2, "common": { "model_name": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "max_turns": 10, "num_agents": 3 }, "runs": [ { "input_json_file_path": "path/to/data1.json", "output_json_file_path": "path/to/output1.json", "task_instruction_prompt": "Instruction for run 1" }, { "input_json_file_path": "path/to/data2.json", "output_json_file_path": "path/to/output2.json", "task_instruction_prompt": "Instruction for run 2", "model_name": "gpt-4", "max_turns": 15 } ] } ``` In this example, the second run overrides the `model_name` and `max_turns` settings from the common configuration. 2. **Ensure all required dependencies are installed.** ### Running the Batch Executor To run the batch executor, use the following command from the terminal: `mallm-batch path/to/your/batch_config.json` ### Behavior - The batch executor will process each run configuration in the order they appear in the JSON file. - For each run: - It will create a `Config` object by merging the common settings with the run-specific settings. - It will then initialize a `Scheduler` with this configuration and run it. - Progress and any errors will be printed to the console. - If a configuration is invalid or encounters an error during execution, the batch processor will skip to the next run. - The process continues until all runs have been attempted. ### Tips - Place settings that are common to most or all runs in the `common` section to reduce repetition. - Run-specific settings will override common settings if both are specified. - Always test your configurations individually before running them in a batch to ensure they work as expected. - Use descriptive output file names to easily identify the results of each run. - Monitor the console output for any error messages or skipped configurations. By using the batch executor with common settings, you can easily manage multiple experiments or process various datasets with shared parameters, saving time and reducing the chance of configuration errors. ## Contributing If you want to contribute, please use this pre-commit hook to ensure the same formatting for everyone. ```bash pip install pre-commit pre-commit install ``` ### Testing You can run unit tests locally: `pytest ./test/` ## Citation If you use this repository or the [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.22932) for your research work, please cite it in the following way. ``` @misc{becker2024multiagentlargelanguagemodels, title={Multi-Agent Large Language Models for Conversational Task-Solving}, author={Jonas Becker}, year={2024}, eprint={2410.22932}, archivePrefix={arXiv}, primaryClass={cs.CL}, url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.22932}, } ```

classes.name: mallm.models.discussion.SplitFreeTextResponseGenerator.SplitFreeTextResponseGenerator
documentation:
The SplitFreeTextResponseGenerator class is designed to generate free-form text responses based on the input prompts and tasks.
It makes two queries instead of one: 1) generate the agreement 2) generate the corresponding message depending on the agreement
With this split-up method, the agreement extraction can work reliably.

classes.name: mallm.models.discussion.FreeTextResponseGenerator.FreeTextResponseGenerator
documentation:
The FreeTextResponseGenerator class is designed to generate free-form text responses based on the input prompts and tasks.
It utilizes a chat interface to interact with the language model and generate responses that are not limited by specific templates or structures.

classes.name: mallm.models.discussion.JSONResponseGenerator.JSONResponseGenerator
documentation:
The JSONResponseGenerator is responsible for generating responses in JSON format for discussions.
The responses are formatted according to a specific JSON schema, ensuring consistency and structure in the output.
With this split-up method, the agreement extraction can work reliably.

classes.name: mallm.models.discussion.SimpleResponseGenerator.SimpleResponseGenerator
documentation:
The SimpleResponseGenerator class is designed to generate simple, straightforward responses based on the input prompts and tasks.
Its prompts are simplified to mitigate bloating the context length and not distacting the LLM from what is important.

modules.name: mallm.models
documentation:
MALLM - Multi-Agent Large Language Models.

A python library for collaborative agents.

classes.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat._convert_input
documentation: Overwrite to send direct chat structure to tgi endpoint

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat.generate_prompt
documentation: Overwrite to send direct chat structure to tgi endpoint

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat._call
documentation:
Run the LLM on the given input.

Override this method to implement the LLM logic.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt to generate from.
    stop: Stop words to use when generating. Model output is cut off at the
        first occurrence of any of the stop substrings.
        If stop tokens are not supported consider raising NotImplementedError.
    run_manager: Callback manager for the run.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary additional keyword arguments. These are usually passed
        to the model provider API call.

Returns:
    The model output as a string. Actual completions SHOULD NOT include the prompt.

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat._stream
documentation:
Stream the LLM on the given prompt.

This method should be overridden by subclasses that support streaming.

If not implemented, the default behavior of calls to stream will be to
fallback to the non-streaming version of the model and return
the output as a single chunk.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt to generate from.
    stop: Stop words to use when generating. Model output is cut off at the
        first occurrence of any of these substrings.
    run_manager: Callback manager for the run.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary additional keyword arguments. These are usually passed
        to the model provider API call.

Returns:
    An iterator of GenerationChunks.

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat._identifying_params
documentation: Return a dictionary of identifying parameters.

methods.name: mallm.models.Chat.Chat._llm_type
documentation: Get the type of language model used by this chat model. Used for logging purposes only.

A custom chat model that queries the chat API of HuggingFace Text Generation Inference

When contributing an implementation to LangChain, carefully document
the model including the initialization parameters, include
an example of how to initialize the model and include any relevant
links to the underlying models documentation or API.

Example:

    .. code-block:: python

        model = CustomChatModel(n=2)
        result = model.invoke([HumanMessage(content="hello")])
        result = model.batch([[HumanMessage(content="hello")],
                             [HumanMessage(content="world")]])

classes.name: mallm.models.personas.IPIPPersonaGenerator.IPIPPersonaGenerator
documentation: The IPIPPersonaGenerator class is a specialized PersonaGenerator designed to generate personas based on the Big Five personality traits. It is capable of identifying the necessary participants, their roles, and characteristics to foster a rich and diverse discussion.

classes.name: mallm.models.personas.NoPersonaGenerator.NoPersonaGenerator
documentation: The NoPersonaGenerator class is used to create agents without a persona.

methods.name: mallm.models.personas.PersonaGenerator.PersonaGenerator.generate_persona
documentation:
Abstract method to generate a list of persona descriptions based on the given task.

Parameters:
task_description (str): Description of the task for which personas are to be generated.
num_agents (int): Number of persona descriptions to generate.

Returns:
list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a persona
                       with keys like 'role' and 'persona' (or other relevant descriptors),
                       each mapped to their respective string description.

classes.name: mallm.models.personas.MockGenerator.MockGenerator
documentation: The MockGenerator class is a placeholder PersonaGenerator that generates a simple persona based on the number of already generated personas..

classes.name: mallm.models.personas.ExpertGenerator.ExpertGenerator
documentation: The ExpertGenerator class is a specialized PersonaGenerator designed to generate expert personas for a given task or discussion. It is capable of identifying the necessary participants, their roles, and descriptions to foster a rich and informative discussion.

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_response
documentation:
Abstract method to generate an agents response to a discussion.

Parameters:
current_prompt (list[dict[str, str]]): The prompt for the LM.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.
agreement (Optional[bool]): the agreement if already computed.
baseline (bool): Whether use the prompt for the baseline, without discussion.
drafting (bool): Whether the response should be drafting a new solution.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_baseline
documentation:
Abstract method to generate the response of a single LM as a baseline.

Parameters:
task_instruction (str): The instruction for the task and appended context.
input_str (str): The input for the task.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_ablation
documentation:
Abstract method to generate the response of a single LM that iteratively improves on the current solution.

Parameters:
task_instruction (str): The instruction for the task and appended context.
input_str (str): The input for the task.
current_solution: (str): The current solution to improve.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_feedback
documentation:
Abstract method to generate feedback to improve a draft for a discussion.

Parameters:
data (TemplateFilling): The fields used for prompting the LM.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_improve
documentation:
Abstract method to generate feedback and an improved solution for a discussion.

Parameters:
data (TemplateFilling): The fields used for prompting the LM.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.generate_draft
documentation:
Abstract method to generate a draft for a discussion.

Parameters:
data (TemplateFilling): The fields used for prompting the LM.
chain_of_thought (bool): Whether to use Zero-Shot CoT.

Returns:
Response: An object with the attributes "agreement", "message", and "solution".

methods.name: mallm.models.discussion.ResponseGenerator.ResponseGenerator.extract_agreement
documentation:
Determines whether a string given by an agent means an agreement or disagreement.
Returns bool

Evaluate the generated text against a reference text.

reference_text (list[str]): The reference text to compare against.

Returns:
dict[str, Any]: The evaluation results.

methods.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.metric.Metric.name
documentation:
Return the name of the evaluation metric.

Returns:
str: The name of the metric.

Abstract class for evaluation metrics in text generation tasks.
This class provides a template for implementing various evaluation metrics.

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.bertscore.BERTScore
documentation:
A class to evaluate the BERTScore for text generation tasks.
This includes precision, recall, and F1 score.

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.qa.MultiChoiceBoolean
documentation: A class to evaluate the accuracy on multiple choice/QA tasks.

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.qa.AnswerabilityBoolean
documentation: A class to evaluate the answerability accuracy on QA tasks that include non-answerable questions (i.e., no reference).

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.qa.SquadScore
documentation: A class to evaluate the accuracy on squad like tasks that include non-answerable questions (i.e., no reference).

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.qa.IncludesAnswer
documentation: A class to evaluate the accuracy on squad like tasks that include non-answerable questions (i.e., no reference).

classes.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.ifeval.IFEval

methods.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.ifeval.IFEval.read_prompt_list
documentation: Read inputs from jsonl.

methods.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.ifeval.IFEval.test_instruction_following_strict
documentation: Tests response to see if instrutions are followed.

methods.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.ifeval.IFEval.test_instruction_following_loose
documentation: Tests response for an upper bound for following instructions.

methods.name: mallm.evaluation.metrics.ifeval.IFEval.read_prompt_to_response_dict
documentation: Creates dictionary matching prompt and response.
A class to evaluate generated texts by instruction-following, following https://github.com/google-research/google-research/blob/master/instruction_following_eval/evaluation_main.py

classes.name: mallm.discourse_policy.collective_refinement.CollectiveRefinement
documentation:
A discussion protocol where agents improve their answers through multiple rounds of feedback.

In this protocol, each agent first generates an answer independently. In each following round,
every agent receives the answers from all other agents at the same time. Using this shared information,
each agent refines their own answer. This process continues over several rounds, helping agents
gradually reach a shared or improved solution.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.approval_voting.ApprovalVoting
documentation: The Approval Voting decision protocol allows panelists to approve any number of solutions after a certain number of turns.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.consensus_voting.ConsensusVoting
documentation: The Voting decision protocol allows panelists to vote for the best answer after a certain number of turns.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.consensus.HybridMajorityConsensus
documentation:
The Hybrid Majority Consensus imitates the implementation by Yin et. al.
Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.01823

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.simple_voting.SimpleVoting
documentation: The Voting decision protocol allows panelists to vote for the best answer after a certain number of turns.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.summary.Summary
documentation: The Summary decision protocol creates a summary of all answers after a certain number of turns.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.ranked_voting.RankedVoting
documentation: The Ranked Voting decision protocol allows panelists to rank their preferences among a set of answers after a certain number of turns.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.cumulative_voting.CumulativeVoting
documentation:
The Cumulative Voting decision protocol allows panelists to distribute 10 points among the solutions.
The solution with the highest total points is selected as the final decision.

classes.name: mallm.decision_protocol.protocol.DecisionProtocol

methods.name: mallm.decision_protocol.protocol.DecisionProtocol.remove_duplicate_answers
documentation: Removes duplicate entries from the answer choices, even if they have different capitalization or slightly different writing in multiple-choice format.

methods.name: mallm.decision_protocol.protocol.DecisionProtocol.make_decision
documentation:
Abstract method to make a decision based on agreements, the current turn number, and the list of panelists.

Parameters:
agreements (list[dict[str, any]]): A list of agreement objects from agents.
turn (int): The current turn number.

Returns:
str, bool: str is the result of the conversation and bool describes whether they agreed or not.

Abstract base class for a decision protocol in a multi-agent LLM framework.
Any concrete decision protocol must implement the make_decision method.

classes.name: mallm.agents.panelist.Panelist

methods.name: mallm.agents.panelist.Panelist.participate
documentation: Either calls feedback() or improve(), depending on whether this panelist is allowed to draft solutions or not.

Represents a Panelist agent, a type of Agent that specializes in participating in discussions.
It is prompted to either improve the current solution or provide feedback.

classes.name: mallm.agents.draftProposer.DraftProposer

methods.name: mallm.agents.draftProposer.DraftProposer.__init__
documentation: Initializes a DraftProposer agent with the necessary components for facilitating discussions.

Represents a DraftProposer agent, a type of Agent that specializes in proposing drafts for discussions.
It is prompted to remain neutral and will not be included in the decision-making.

classes.name: mallm.agents.policyFeedback.PolicyFeedback

methods.name: mallm.agents.policyFeedback.PolicyFeedback.__init__
documentation: Initializes a PolicyFeedback agent with the necessary components.

methods.name: mallm.agents.policyFeedback.PolicyFeedback.policy_feedback
documentation: Agent provides feedback on how to improve discussion to fit the given policy.

Represents a PolicyFeedback agent, a type of Agent that ensures that the other agents adhere to a given policy.
This agent does not contribute to solving the task.

classes.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent

documentation: Initializes an Agent with the necessary components for facilitating discussions.

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.improve
documentation: Agent improves a solution based on the current state of the discussion.

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.draft
documentation: Agent drafts a solution based on the current state of the discussion.

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.feedback
documentation: Agent provides feedback to a solution based on the current state of the discussion.

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.update_memory
documentation: Updates the memory of this agent with another discussion entry.

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.get_memories
documentation: Retrieves memory data from the agents memory

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.get_own_messages
documentation:
Retrieves memory from the agents memory bucket as a string
context_length refers to the amount of turns the agent can use as rationale
Returns: list of strings

methods.name: mallm.agents.agent.Agent.get_discussion_history
documentation:
Retrieves memory from the agents memory as a string
context_length refers to the amount of turns the agent can memorize the previous discussion
Represents an Agent in the discussion, capable of improving, drafting, and providing feedback.

classes.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator

methods.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator.init_agents
documentation:
Instantiates the agents by
1) identify helpful personas depending on the agent_generator
2) create agents with the personas

methods.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator.update_memories
documentation: Updates the memories of all declared agents.

methods.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator.discuss
documentation:
The routine responsible for the discussion between agents to solve a task.

The routine is organized as follows:
1) Create agents with personas
2) Discuss the problem based on the given paradigm (iteratively check for agreement between agents)
3) After max turns or agreement reached: return the final result to the task sample

Returns final response, global memory, agent specific memory, turns needed, last agreements of agents, discussion time in seconds, boolean if agreement was reached

methods.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator.get_memories
documentation: Retrieves memory data from the agents memory

methods.name: mallm.coordinator.Coordinator.get_discussion_history
documentation:
Retrieves memory from the agents memory as a string
context_length refers to the amount of turns the agent can memorize the previous discussion

The Coordinator is responsible for managing the discussion process between agents.
It initializes agents based on the provided configuration.
The coordinator facilitates the discussion by allowing agents to draft, improve, and provide feedback on solutions.
It also keeps track of the discussion history and agreements reached during the process.

functions.name: mallm.utils.utils.pretty_print_dict
documentation:
Pretty prints a dictionary in a readable and styled format.

Parameters:
config_dict (dict): The dictionary to be pretty printed.
indent (int): The number of spaces to use for indentation.
width (int): The maximum number of characters per line.

functions.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions_registry.conflict_make
documentation:
Makes sure if A conflicts with B, B will conflict with A.

Args:
    conflicts: Dictionary of potential conflicts where key is instruction id
        and value is set of instruction ids that it conflicts with.

Returns:
    Revised version of the dictionary. All instructions conflict with
    themselves. If A conflicts with B, B will conflict with A.

classes.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.run_discussion
documentation: Runs a single discussion between agents on a sample.

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.manage_discussions
documentation:
Manages all discussions on the data.
Discussions are handled in a queue of length max_concurrent_requests.
Once a spot in the queue is free because a discussion ended, the next discussion is initialized.

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.run_ablation
documentation: Run an ablation where a single LM iteratively improves the solution the same number of times as the multi-agent system.

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.run_baseline
documentation: Task a single LM to solve a sample.

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.manage_baseline
documentation:
Manages all samples of the data.
The LM answers the query with a single request with no discussion being held.

methods.name: mallm.scheduler.Scheduler.run
documentation: The routine that runs the discussions between LLM agents on the provided data.

The Scheduler is responsible for managing the flow of all discussions.
It oversees the reading and processing of input data and initializes the coordinator for each discussion.
The Scheduler is capable of managing many discussions simultaneously through parallelized API requests.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.Instruction
documentation: An instruction template.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ResponseLanguageChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ResponseLanguageChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    language: A string representing the expected language of the response. The
        language has to comply to the 97 types defined in
        `langid.py` (https://pypi.org/project/langid/1.1.5/), which follows
        ISO 639-1 codes (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes);
        for example, `en` for English, `zh` for Chinese, `fr` for French.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ResponseLanguageChecker.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ResponseLanguageChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ResponseLanguageChecker.check_following
documentation:
Check if the language of the entire response follows the instruction.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response.

Returns:
    True if the language of `value` follows instruction; otherwise False.
Check the language of the entire response.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfSentences

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfSentences.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    num_sentences: An integer specifying the number of sentences as a
        threshold.
    relation: A string in (`less than`, `at least`), defining the relational
        operator for comparison.
        Two relational comparisons are supported for now:
        if 'less than', the actual number of sentences < the threshold;
        if 'at least', the actual number of sentences >= the threshold.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfSentences.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfSentences.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfSentences.check_following
documentation:
Check if the number of sentences follows the instruction.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response.

Returns:
    True if the response follows the instruction.

Raise:
        ValueError if the string in `instruction_args` is not in
        [`less_than`, `at_least`].
Check the number of sentences.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PlaceholderChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PlaceholderChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:

    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PlaceholderChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PlaceholderChecker.check_following
documentation:
Check if the number of placeholders follows the instruction.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response.

Returns:
    True if the actual number of placeholders in the response is greater than
    or equal to `num_placeholders`; otherwise, False.
Check the placeholders in template writing.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.BulletListChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    num_bullets: An integer specifying the exact number of bullet lists
        that is required to appear in the response.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.BulletListChecker.check_following
documentation:
Check if the number of bullet lists meets the requirement.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response. The response is expected to
        contain some bullet lists that start with `\*`.

Returns:
    True if the actual number of bullet lists in the response meets the
    requirement.
Checks the bullet list in the prompt.

documentation:
Checks if the number of highlighted sections meets the requirement.

Args:
    value: a string repesenting the response. The response is expected to
        contain highlighted sections in the format of *highlighted*.

Returns:
    True if the actual number of highlighted sections in the format of
    *highlighed sections* meets the minimum requirement; otherwise False.
Checks the highlighted section.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.SectionChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.SectionChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    section_spliter: A string represents the section spliter keyword that
        marks a new section, i.e., `Section` or `SECTION`.
    num_sections: An integer specifying the number of sections.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.SectionChecker.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.SectionChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.SectionChecker.check_following
documentation:
Checks the response contains multiple sections.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response. The response is expected
        to contain multiple sections (number of sections is greater than 1).
        A new section starts with `Section 1`, where the number denotes the
        section index.

Returns:
    True if the number of sections in the response is greater than or equal to
    the minimum number of sections; otherwise, False.
Checks the sections.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    num_paragraphs: An integer specifying the number of paragraphs.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphChecker.check_following
documentation:
Checks the response contains required number of paragraphs.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response. The response may contain
        paragraphs that are separated by the markdown divider: `***`.

Returns:
    True if the actual number of paragraphs is the same as required;
    otherwise, False.
Checks the paragraphs.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PostscriptChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PostscriptChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

    postscript_marker: A string containing the keyword that marks the start
        of the postscript section.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PostscriptChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.PostscriptChecker.check_following
documentation:
Checks if the response follows the postscript format.

Args:
    value: a string representing the response. The response is expected to
        contain a postscript section.

Returns:
    True if the response contains a postscript section starting with
    the keyword containing in the `instruction_args`; otherwise False.
Checks the postscript.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    original_message: A string representing the original message. The
        rephrased response should only change its words/sentences in between
        its two asterisks, for example, *change me*. Both original and rephrased
        messages should contain the changes in the form of *change me*.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.check_following
documentation:
Checks if the rephrasing follows the instruction.

Args:
    value: A string representing the response, which is expected to rephras
        the string of `instruction_args`.

Returns:
    True if `value` and `instruction_args` only differ by the words/sentences
    in between two asterisks such as *change me*; otherwise, False.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.is_change
documentation: Check if there is change in the response in the form of *change me*.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseChecker.strip_changes
documentation: Strips off the changes.
Checks the repharse.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    keywords: A sequence of strings representing the keywords that are
        expected in the response.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordChecker.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordChecker.check_following
documentation: Check if the response contain the expected keywords.
Check the exisitence of certain keywords.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordFrequencyChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordFrequencyChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    keyword: A string representing a keyword that is expected in the response.
    frequency: An integer specifying the number of times `keyword` is expected
        to appear in the response.
    relation: A string in (`less than`, `at least`), defining the relational
        operator for comparison.
        Two relational comparisons are supported for now:
        if 'less than', the actual number of occurrences < frequency;
        if 'at least', the actual number of occurrences >= frequency.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordFrequencyChecker.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordFrequencyChecker.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeywordFrequencyChecker.check_following
documentation: Checks if the response contain the keyword with required frequency.
Check the keyword frequency.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfWords

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfWords.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    num_words: An integer specifying the number of words contained in the
        response.
    relation: A string in (`less than`, `at least`), defining the relational
        operator for comparison.
        Two relational comparisons are supported for now:
        if 'less than', the actual number of words < num_words;
        if 'at least', the actual number of words >= num_words.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfWords.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfWords.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.NumberOfWords.check_following
documentation: Checks if the response contains the expected number of words.
Checks the number of words.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.JsonFormat.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.JsonFormat.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.
Check the Json format.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphFirstWordCheck

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphFirstWordCheck.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    num_paragraphs: An integer indicating the number of paragraphs expected
        in the response. A paragraph is a subset of the string that is
        expected to be separated by '\n\n'.
    nth_paragraph: An integer indicating the paragraph number that we look at.
        Note that n starts from 1.
    first_word: A string that represent the first word of the bth paragraph.

Returns:
    A string representing the instruction description.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphFirstWordCheck.get_instruction_args
documentation: Returns the keyward args of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphFirstWordCheck.get_instruction_args_keys
documentation: Returns the args keys of `build_description`.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ParagraphFirstWordCheck.check_following
documentation:
Checks for required number of paragraphs and correct first word.

Args:
    value: a string representing the response. The response may contain
        paragraphs that are separated by two new lines and the first word of
        the nth paragraph will have to match a specified word.

Returns:
    True if the number of paragraphs is the same as required and the first
    word of the specified paragraph is the same as required. Otherwise, false.
Check the paragraph and the first word of the nth paragraph.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeySentenceChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.KeySentenceChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

TODO(jeffrey) add relation - at least/at most?

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.ForbiddenWords.build_description

Builds the instruction description.

Args:
    original_paragraph: A string presenting the original paragraph. The
        rephrases response should have betweeb low-high words in common.

    high: An integer representing the upper bound of similar words.

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.RephraseParagraph.get_instruction_args

documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:

    let_frequency: An integer specifying the number of times `keyword` is

    let_relation: A string in (`less than`, `at least`), defining the

        supported for now; if 'less than', the actual number of
        occurrences < frequency; if 'at least', the actual number of
        occurrences >= frequency.

    A string representing the instruction description.

classes.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.CapitalWordFrequencyChecker

methods.name: mallm.utils.evaluation.instructions.CapitalWordFrequencyChecker.build_description
documentation:
Build the instruction description.

Args:
    capital_frequency: An integer that represents the number of words that

    capital_relation: A string that is 'at least' or 'at most' that refers to
